mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing equity investors to price in lower earnings growth and driving negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60d returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances when UNRATE crosses above 4.5 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal spending and stable inflation indicates robust consumer demand without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, creating a favorable earnings environment that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or positive returns occur in less than 50 percent of triggered cases when RSAFS growth exceeds CPIAUCSL growth by more than 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding corporate capacity utilization and near-term earnings revisions, which drives short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the following week, but only during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during rate-hiking cycles, or 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive when INDPRO month-over-month change exceeds 0.3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained economic expansion and wage growth, which supports corporate revenue trajectories and generates positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive 60d returns occur in less than 55 percent of periods with positive PAYEMS growth.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.3 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and consumer confidence, which reduces near-term economic momentum and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20d returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances when HOUST drops below 1300, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation runs persistently above the federal funds rate, real interest rates turn negative, eroding purchasing power and forcing eventual aggressive tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive power in multivariate regression, or positive 60d returns dominate when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 1.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent confirms strong macroeconomic momentum, prompting institutional portfolio rebalancing into equities and generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns, but only when labor markets remain tight.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns when UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 over any 2-year rolling window.
